## New Research

### Swiss Re / Insurance Journal (Mar 23)
- Record 92% of 2025 nat cat insured losses (US$107B) driven by secondary perils
- 2026 forecast: US$148B baseline, US$320B peak-loss scenario
- Secondary perils (wildfires, SCS, floods) drove 92% of 2025 losses

### Aon Catastrophe Insight — SCS Surpassed Tropical Cyclones
- Severe convective storms (SCS) overtook tropical cyclones as costliest insurance peril of the 21st century
- US SCS losses expected to grow in 2026

### EIOPA Makes a Splash in EU Natcat Risk Pool Debate
- EIOPA emerged as significant voice in pan-European natcat risk pool debate
- Addresses how EU-wide pool could manage escalating climate-related cat losses and narrow protection gap
- Implications for regulatory capital requirements and risk-sharing mechanisms
- Source: https://www.insuranceerm.com/analysis/2026/04/09/eiopa-makes-a-splash-in-eu-natcat-risk-pool-debate/ | Apr 9, 2026

### Insurance Talent Gap as Strategic Risk
- Growing talent shortage in insurance/reinsurance escalated to strategic-level business risk
- Skills gap impairing insurers' ability to model, price, manage climate risks
- Direct implications for actuarial function resourcing and future pipeline
